description: Named MCP exposure profiles control which tools and resources the AINL MCP server registers at startup. They scope what MCP hosts can discover and call. They do NOT replace security profiles (runtime adapter restrictions) or capability grants (execution authorization).

validate_only.description: Read-only validation and compilation; no execution, security-report, or resources. Recommended for ultra-narrow validation-only usage (e.g. desktop-bound or Cowork/Dispatch-style agents).

inspect_only.description: Read-only inspection: validation, compilation, capabilities, and security reports; no execution. Recommended default for desktop-bound, Cowork/Dispatch-style, and read-only inspection-first environments.

safe_workflow.description: Full read-only tooling plus safe-default execution. All resources exposed. Use only after explicit operator review of grants, policies, limits, and adapter exposure.

full.description: All tools and resources. Equivalent to no exposure restriction.
